CShopDlg::OnPacket#ShopDispatch.notes: NPC_SHOP (v95 opcode 66/0x42) op-byte dispatcher. The shop transaction senders (CShopDlg::SendBuyRequest@0x6e9bb0, SendSellRequest@0x6e7260, SendRechargeRequest@0x6e4e90) each build COutPacket(66) with a leading Encode1(op) discriminator (0=BUY @0x6ea5c1, 1=SELL @0x6e7465, 2=RECHARGE @0x6e4fe3) then the per-op body. CSummoned::SetDamaged.note: DAMAGE_SUMMON send (CMSG 209/0xD1). CSummoned::SetDamaged@0x74b730: COutPacket(209)@0x74bb6a + Encode4(summonId)@0x74bb82, then (mob-present path) Encode1(nAttackIdx)@0x74bbae + Encode4(damage)@0x74bbb8 + Encode4(mobTemplateId)@0x74bbd8 + Encode1(nDir<0)@0x74bbed. (No-mob path sends Encode1(0xFE)+Encode4(damage).) v87 SetDamaged@0x7f879a is byte-identical (the dir<0 byte and the 0xFE no-mob branch are present in v83/v87 too, not v95-only). Atlas serverbound Damage (corrected wire) decodes summonId+attackIdx+damage, then on the mob-present branch monsterIdFrom(templateId)+dir<0 byte (consumed, not surfaced); the 0xFE sentinel selects the no-mob branch (damage only). The mob/no-mob branch is a data-dependent discriminator a flat positional diff cannot model, so the cell is flat-diff-invalid (tier-1), verified by byte-level mob/no-mob decode tests. v95 reference (PDB build).

CSummoned::TryDoingAttackManual.note: SUMMON_ATTACK send (CMSG 208/0xD0). CSummoned::TryDoingAttackManual@0x751240: COutPacket(208)@0x75226b + Encode4(summonId)@0x752287 + Encode4(~drInfo0)@0x75229b + Encode4(~drInfo1)@0x7522af + Encode4(updateTime)@0x7522c0 + Encode4(~drInfo2)@0x7522d4 + Encode4(~drInfo3)@0x7522e8 + Encode1(action&0x7F|bLeft<<7)@0x752302 + Encode4(dwKey)@0x752325 + Encode4(crc32)@0x75234c + Encode1(nMobCount)@0x75235c + Encode2(userX)@0x7523a5 + Encode2(userY)@0x7523dd + Encode2(summonX)@0x75240a + Encode2(summonY)@0x752438 + Encode4(repeatSkillPoint)@0x752450 + per-mob loop{Encode4(mobId)@0x7524ac + Encode4(templateId)@0x7524cc + Encode1(hitAction)@0x7524e2 + Encode1(foreAction|left<<7)@0x75250c + Encode1(frameIdx)@0x752522 + Encode1(calcDmgStatIdx)@0x75253b + Encode2(x)@0x75256c + Encode2(y)@0x7525a0 + Encode2(x)@0x7525d3 + Encode2(y)@0x752607 + Encode2(tDelay)@0x75261d + Encode4(damage)@0x752632} + Encode4(skillCRC)@0x75266f. Atlas serverbound Attack (corrected wire) decodes this exact layout: summonId + (GMS>=87) anti-hack envelope (drInfo/updateTime/dwKey/crc32) + action + count + positions + (GMS>=95) repeatSkillPoint, then the per-target loop and skillCRC trailer.
